# Schedule Requirements Checklist: UC-22 Generate Possible Class Schedules

**Purpose**: Validate the completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurability of the UC-22 schedule-generation requirements before implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/thaia/ECE493Group19/specs/022-branch-schedule-generator/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the student inputs required before schedule generation fully specified, including term selection, course selection, and optional constraints? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK002 Are the minimum details required for each displayed schedule option fully documented beyond days, times, and sections if comparison depends on additional fields? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for invalid input correction, including empty selections and duplicate courses, as a distinct user-visible outcome? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Are dependency requirements for planning-term, catalog, and timetable data availability explicitly documented and bounded? [Completeness, Spec §Dependencies, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-003a]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the term "feasible schedule" defined with criteria specific enough to distinguish valid schedules from conflicting or constraint-violating schedules? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Phase 0]
- [x] CHK006 Are "optional scheduling constraints" described precisely enough that reviewers can tell which constraint types are in scope for UC-22? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK007 Is "consistent order" for unranked schedule options clear enough to avoid conflicting interpretations across requirements and acceptance scenarios? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005a, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK008 Is the phrase "first user-visible result" tied to a concrete observable event for feasible schedule generation? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-002, Spec §SC-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are the feasible, partial-continuation, no-feasible-schedule, retrieval-failure, and invalid-input states described consistently between user stories, functional requirements, and non-functional messaging requirements? [Consistency, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Spec §FR-010, Spec §NFR-004]
- [x] CHK010 Do planning-only requirements consistently prohibit registration changes across user stories, functional requirements, and success criteria? [Consistency, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK011 Are the boundaries between unschedulable-course continuation and no-feasible-schedule outcomes consistent across the acceptance scenarios and edge cases? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Are all success criteria objectively measurable without introducing implementation-specific metrics or internal system details? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK013 Do the acceptance scenarios define the observable outcomes strongly enough to distinguish complete success, partial success, and failure conditions? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK014 Is the 95% performance target scoped clearly enough to show whether it applies only to feasible requests or also to failure and recovery states? [Measurability, Spec §NFR-002, Spec §SC-005]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are primary, alternate, and exception flows all represented in the written requirements for feasible generation, continuation after unschedulable courses, and no-result/error outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK016 Does the spec define what happens when all selected courses are unschedulable versus when courses are schedulable but no valid combination exists? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK017 Are retry or recovery expectations defined for retrieval failures and long-running generation attempts, or intentionally excluded? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK018 Are accessibility requirements specified for both the input workflow and every distinct schedule-generation result state? [Coverage, Spec §NFR-001, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK019 Are privacy requirements explicit enough to define whether generated schedules and selected constraints are visible only to the requesting student in all states? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-003]
- [x] CHK020 Are error-message distinction requirements measurable enough to verify that unschedulable continuation, infeasible results, and retrieval failures cannot be confused? [Measurability, Spec §NFR-004]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK021 Are the assumptions about pre-authenticated students, external catalog/timetable data, and planning-only behavior validated against the functional requirements rather than left implicit? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §Requirements]
- [x] CHK022 Does the spec define the expected behavior when external planning-term, catalog, or timetable dependencies are unavailable before generation begins? [Dependency, Spec §Dependencies, Spec §FR-003a, Spec §FR-009]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- Use this checklist to review the requirements text, not implementation behavior.
